title: Regolith Relay Dispersion on the Selene Grid
seed_problem: place m grid points maximizing the minimum pairwise L1 distance subject to every demand cell being within radius R of a point (max-min dispersion x coverage)
mutation: composite: keep the hard L1 coverage constraint and the max-min dispersion objective, then multiply Dmin by a number-theoretic resonance factor (1 + Align/m) where a pod is resonant iff (a*x+b*y) mod K == r0; the residue bonus is non-degenerate (a spread pod can be snapped onto a ley line for free) so it adds a genuine second mechanism and strategy divergence without collapsing dispersion
strategies: reference/naive: greedy cover then fill spares with demand cells in input order (clustered near hubs, Dmin ~ demand offset)
pure-dispersion greedy: greedy cover then one-pass farthest-point spread of spares into the empty basin; high Dmin, resonance left at chance level
resonance-aware spread: farthest-point spread with each spare snapped onto the nearest free (a*x+b*y)==r0 ley-line cell; keeps Dmin high and drives Align (and the 1+Align/m factor) toward its maximum
coverage-budget balancing: on trap instances, invest scarce pods to both cover the crowded quadrant and reach far demand while spreading the remainder
